title: Neroli and lime wax melts
subtitle: Bright, floral-citrus wax tarts for a melt burner
excerpt: Soy wax melts scented with neroli and lime essential oils. Intended for use in an oil burner or electric wax melt warmer. The neroli gives a warm floral note; the lime adds a sharp citrus brightness. Makes 6 small tarts.
sourceNotes: Neroli essential oil flash point: typically above 60°C. Lime essential oil (steam-distilled): flash point approximately 52 to 60°C depending on the batch. Both can be added safely at the pour temperature for soy wax melts (55 to 60°C). Soy wax melt fragrance load: up to 12% for melt tarts (melts are designed to release fragrance at a lower temperature than container candles; higher fragrance loads are appropriate). National Candle Association guidelines.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Fragrance load stays at 10% of wax weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 2 hours to set fully before removing from the tray.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in a cool dark place. Wax melts keep 2 years. The lime top note fades faster than the neroli base; after 12 months the fragrance may be primarily neroli. The wax itself does not degrade.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which an essential oil can ignite near a flame. Lime essential oil has one of the lower flash points of common fragrance oils; add it at the lower end of the pour temperature range.
The temperature at which wax fully liquefies. Soy wax melts are designed to release fragrance at the low temperature of a tealight burner or electric warmer, typically 40 to 55°C.

content.text: Wax melts release fragrance in a warming dish without a direct flame in the scent medium itself. Because the wax melts at a lower temperature than a candle burns, a higher fragrance load is possible: 10% of wax weight rather than 6 to 8% for jar candles. Neroli is warm and floral with a slight honey quality; lime adds sharpness. Both have
values above the pour temperature of 55 to 60°C for soy wax melts; add both at the cooler end of the window. Soy wax melts have a low
(40 to 50°C) and will soften in a warm room; store in a cool place to prevent tarts fusing together in the packaging.

items.prepNote: Container blend soy wax (a slightly softer wax is fine for melts)
7% of wax weight; warm, floral note
3.5% of wax weight; sharp citrus; steam-distilled only

content.text: Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher over low heat. Do not leave unattended. Remove from heat once fully melted. Let the wax cool to 55 to 60°C on the probe thermometer. Add neroli first, then lime essential oil, and stir to combine. Pour into the individual cavities of the silicone ice cube tray. Leave undisturbed for at least 2 hours until fully set. The melts will be solid and opaque when ready; pop them out of the tray by pressing gently on the base.

Place one melt in the dish of a tealight burner or an electric wax warmer. A single 10 g tart will fragrance a medium-sized room for 3 to 4 hours. When the fragrance is spent, the wax can be poured into the bin (once cool) and replaced with a new tart. Do not leave a tealight burner unattended.

Store in a cool dark place in a sealed bag or container. Wax melts keep 2 years. Keep away from heat sources; the low melt point means they can soften and stick together in warm storage. The lime top note fades over time.
